description: Absorption-justification audit schema covering manifest completeness, parity evidence, branch coverage, last-resort justification, restore-command validity, registry integration, and deletion-gate tooling. Max score: 14.

pillars.name: manifest-completeness
pillars.check: ABSORPTION_MATRIX contains all 5 mandatory columns (Source, Target, Status, Last-Resort-Exceptions, Restore-Command) and any of the optional sections are present where applicable

pillars.name: parity-evidence
pillars.check: ABSORPTION_MATRIX includes explicit Target Evidence column with at least one citation (commit SHA, file path, artifact reference, or test run) per non-NO_MERIT row

pillars.name: branch-coverage
pillars.check: Every branch in source repo with unique content (not already merged to main) has a corresponding row in ABSORPTION_MATRIX with Source, Target, Status, and Restore-Command populated

pillars.name: last-resort-justification
pillars.check: Each Last-Resort-Exceptions entry contains explicit rebuttal addressing: why the target cannot absorb the exception, what residual gap remains, and why archival-not-deletion is insufficient

pillars.name: restore-command-validity
pillars.check: Restore-Command is verifiable: either `mv .archive/X .` for local restoration OR `git clone <remote-url>` plus a recorded bundle SHA-256 checksum that matches the archived artifact

pillars.name: registry-integration
pillars.check: Project card exists at registry/projects/<Name>.json with status field set to `archived` or `active`, absorbed_into field populated when applicable, and/or disposition set to AFFIRM or ARCHIVE_ONLY per disposition-index

pillars.name: deletion-gate-tooling-coverage
pillars.check: Audit references bin/repo-delete-gate.sh OR bin/repo-delete-gate.ps1 in the deletion workflow, OR explicitly explains why the gate is not required (e.g. source already returns 404, manual operator override recorded with justification)

grade_boundaries.classification: On Track: Minor gaps in justification
